author: separatedfromworld
post: Falling in love with depression and OCD. Another nightmare times. Hi, I've got depression and OCD, I'm being treated with sertraline (200mg) for about 0.5 year. I just met someone that I actually fallen in love with. She has anxiety disorder, I think she's been through a hard time becuase of her scars and amount of "close calls" overdosing the pills. After me being this stupid guy before, I once tried to help someone with the same thing. As you can guess this 1st person is still alive becuase of me but she wasn't herself anymore. Now when the next opportunity comes, I just feel like It already happend and I don't want to engage becuase it will end the same way. I just feel worse and worse without talking to her but I also feel like nobody, like someone you couldn't like or even shouldn't like. I don't know how to live with that feeling, I know that I'm a fucking good person to the top, but I also know not everyone is like me and sometimes they will not know the effort put to just being close to someone, to save her life. I'm scared, this time not from OCD and depression, this time becuase of my own memories coming back, this time becuase of love I just can't stand as such emotional person. What should I really do? Help me seek some reasonable answers. I'm not typing this to make myself the worst person on the earth, I just write to get some help organizing my thoughts and my brain. Please help.

author: RegularWeek08
post:
I'm so ashamed of being depressed I'm always convinced that even acknowledging my issues or things that have happened to me in the past is somehow "playing the victim" and I need to be ashamed of it and never bring any of it up. Whenever I do mention any of it I'm so nervous and it makes me extremely uncomfortable. Usually if I bring it up I'm drunk. I guess I always just want someone to tell me that I'm allowed to feel sad, and that I've gone through some shitty stuff. I don't know why I want that validation, and it makes me disgusted with myself that I'm really that person who seeks attention like that. I tried to kill myself in October, and I only told one person and they asked me if I did it for attention from them.

Its just made me so ashamed, and disgusted at myself. I can't tell anyone anything, or I'm terrified that they think I want them to see me as a victim. I'm so alone.

I guess I'm finally learning that this is truly my burden to bear and mine alone.

author: King-ofHearts
post:
Life is a game of chance, not of luck Is it really so terrible to want something in life? To have the knowledge that everything is going to be okay. I don't. Life doesn't teach us this, life teaches us struggle almost every day to either physically, mentally or emotionally get through each and every day until we run out.

Let's be clear, I'm not talking about money, I'm not talking about job security, I'm not even talking about making sure you're safe. I'm talking about you. You ability to see things how they are and to stop overseeing how things might be. We spend every day thinking to ourselves how it would be better if our lives had turned out a different way, with different people in it and with different days. This is not the way things should be.

Life is a test, and a cruel one at that. Whoever tells you that we fall so we can learn to get ourselves up clearly hasn't fallen hard enough. There comes a point where the fight to get back up is a lot harder than the fall itself, so we learn not to climb too high to avoid the risk all together. Say what you want but this is the general way of the world, the "safe" option, when in reality for some people this isn't safe at all. It's survival.

The happiest man alive could have everything he ever wanted, could have done everything he ever wanted to do and could be loved and respected by everyone around him. This man would still no pain, sorrow, betrayal and rage, so wtf is the point?
